summary: In this episode, Groww Mutual Fund's Head of Equities breaks down why India is structurally far stronger than it was 20 years ago, why FII selling has little to do with AI, and how a retail investor should actually think about diversification, active vs passive funds, and fair return expectations. A masterclass in cutting noise from signal.

chapters.label: Why "India story is over" keeps repeating, every cycle
FII selling explained: it's redemption, not the AI trade
Short-term pain: exports, capex, monetary tightening
Where we are today, the turnaround
The long view: the Three C's, Capability, Capacity, Confidence
What worries him: rule of law & fiscal discipline
Should you invest globally? Diversification has a cost
How money is actually made: deep conviction over prediction
Active vs Passive, why the US went passive, and why India won't
Mid cap vs small cap: why the index gap is structural
Optimum diversification across large, mid & small cap
A fund manager's framework: PSS, valuation & the wisdom pyramid
Investing in loss-making IPOs, how FMs think about it
AI: profound technology, but not a magic wand
Closing takeaways: survive 20 years, write your plan, stay disciplined
What's a fair return expectation from equity?

seo.title: Is The India Story Over? - Groww MF's Equity Head Answers
seo.description: In this episode, Groww Mutual Fund's Head of Equities breaks down why India is structurally far stronger than it was 20 years ago, why FII selling has

takeaways: The claim that the India story is over repeats every cycle, but the guest argues India is structurally far stronger than it was 20 years ago.
FII selling is better read as redemption pressure than a bet against India or a move into the AI trade.
Short-term pain from exports, capex and monetary tightening is part of the cycle, and the conversation points to signs of a turnaround.
Investing globally is not free: diversification has a cost, and the episode frames it as a trade-off rather than an obvious upgrade.
The US went passive for its own reasons, and the guest explains why India is not simply following that path, alongside how to think about fair return expectations, discipline and a written plan.
